rationale: Memory leak: 1083 stale timeouts in intervalIds array after firing; implementation bug, no spec rule violated.
hint option silently ignored in cursor.count; CRUD spec requires hint to be forwarded to the count command.

hasNext() missing on AggregationCursor and CommandCursor; API feature addition, not spec conformance.
'SDAM events not correctly propagated' for Mongos and single server; SDAM spec requires events for all topology types.

Mutating caller's insertMany options object; internal implementation bug, no spec rule violated.
Multiple cursor.map() calls only apply last transform; cursor transform chaining bug, not spec behavior.
Callbacks not fired after mongod restart; connection failover implementation bug, no specific spec rule.
GridFS race condition: CheckDone called twice; internal concurrency guard, not spec behavior.
GridStore breaks with Node 8.0.0 fs.read API change; platform compatibility fix, no spec rule.
Replace .skip with $gte in GridFS download.js; performance optimization, no spec conformance issue.

Connection timeout regression after 2.2.26; performance/connection degradation, no spec rule cited.
Driver attempts SCRAM-SHA-1 auth against arbiter nodes; arbiters don't support user authentication per spec.
Username and password must be URI-escaped; connection string spec requires percent-encoding of credentials.
JavaScript heap OOM inserting millions of docs; memory performance issue, no spec rule violated.
ReadPreference class ignored: reads hit primary instead of specified secondary; server-selection spec requires ReadPreference to be honored.
Thin description; _id uniqueness possibly server-side; resolution: Done not Fixed, likely user error.

Incorrect argument documentation for .cursor() method; documentation bug, no behavior change.
Projection on field named 'partial' returns all fields; field name conflicts with internal driver usage, implementation bug.

'TypeError: callback is not a function' under 1000 concurrent queries; internal race condition, no spec rule.
db.dropDatabase reports true but database persists; thin description, likely user environment issue.

updateOne/updateMany don't error on non-atomic update; description cites docs: 'must contain at least one atomic operator'.
